# UC-13.6.4 · Grafana Plugin Installation Health, Version Skew Across HA Cluster Nodes, and Signed-Plugin Provenance Validation

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We compare the small add-ons that draw clocks, maps, and custom panels across every server that answers behind the load balancer. When versions disagree, signatures look wrong, or an add-on fails to load on just one box, we raise a clear signal before teams chase ghosts in the charts.*

---

## Description

Detects Grafana HA plugin plane failures: per-node plugin version skew for the same pluginID behind a load balancer, plugin.loader and plugins/manager errors such as failed to load plugin, unsigned or invalid signature states including signatureOrg drift versus allowlists, post-upgrade compatibility and deprecation warnings in server logs, divergent internal plugin count metrics across replicas, and REST inventory gaps compared to signed bundle provenance expectations.

## Value

Platform teams stop chasing one-node-only panel and app plugin defects during rolling upgrades, prove synchronized marketplace bundles across members, and give risk and engineering governance a tamper-evident ledger that ties plugin versions, publishers, and loader health to configuration change history instead of anecdotal screenshots.

## Implementation

Land grafana:server, grafana:plugins, and grafana:metrics lanes with parsed logger, pluginID, version, signature, signatureType, signatureOrg, host, error, path, and level; poll GET /api/plugins per HA member; forward Prometheus grafana_stat_total_plugins via mstats; publish grafana_ha_node_baseline.csv and grafana_plugin_allowlist.csv; save observability_uc_13_6_4_grafana_ha_plugin_skew_provenance every fifteen minutes over earliest=-4h@h; route high skew and publisher mismatch rows to platform observability on-call; archive weekly evidence exports.

## Evidence

Saved search observability_uc_13_6_4_grafana_ha_plugin_skew_provenance on fifteen-minute schedule; dashboard drilldowns on grafana:server, grafana:plugins, and grafana:metrics; weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab HA pair, install mismatched plugin ZIP versions on two Grafana members, ingest grafana:plugins REST polls and grafana:server loader warnings, and confirm observability_uc_13_6_4_grafana_ha_plugin_skew_provenance returns high severity with skew_flag=1 and non-null recommended_action within thirty minutes.

### Negative scenario

After synchronizing identical signed bundles to every member and restarting Grafana, confirm ver_dc is one for tested pluginIDs, loader_error_flag stays zero, and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to high severity across four consecutive runs.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability platform engineers who operate self-hosted Grafana OSS and Enterprise clusters behind load balancers, Kubernetes StatefulSets or Deployments that pin Grafana replicas, artifact registries that mirror plugin ZIP bundles, and the configuration management teams that promote grafana.ini, provisioning YAML, and container images through GitOps. This use case isolates plugin plane health on highly available Grafana: inconsistent plugin versions across members, plugin loader failures at startup, unsigned or invalid signature states, compatibility errors after Grafana core upgrades, deprecated plugins still referenced by dashboards, and publisher or signatureOrg drift between versions that can indicate supply-chain substitution. It is deliberately not UC-13.6.1 dashboard render latency analytics, not UC-13.6.2 data source proxy and query error analytics, not UC-13.6.3 Unified Alerting scheduler and ngalert evaluation analytics, and not UC-13.6.5 user login audit narratives. It is also not UC-13.6.13 notification channel and contact point delivery failures after alert transitions fire. Here the question is whether every HA member exposes the same plugin binaries, manifests, and signature metadata so panels and app plugins behave identically regardless of which node answers HTTP.

Grafana loads plugins from disk, verifies signatures according to your configured policy, registers them with the plugin loader, and exposes inventory through the HTTP API at /api/plugins when authenticated. Behind HA, operators often mount a shared read-only volume or synchronize plugin directories with rsync, object storage replication, or image-layer baking. Any break in that pipeline yields phantom defects: a clock panel renders on node-a with pluginID grafana-clock-panel at 2.1.0 while node-b still runs 2.0.4, producing JavaScript errors or missing panel types only when the load balancer pins a session to the stale member. Logs from logger=plugin.loader and plugins/manager lines frequently cite failed to load plugin, invalid signature, unsigned plugin warnings, or Angular deprecation notices for legacy app plugins. After Grafana minor upgrades, plugin compatibility gates may refuse older plugin builds until operators refresh bundles, which surfaces as startup errors before dashboards refresh.

Plugin signature verification ties each bundle to a publisher identity Grafana records as signatureOrg and signatureType alongside the semantic version string. When a newer plugin version suddenly reports a different signatureOrg than the prior release documented in your allowlist, treat it as a provenance review trigger rather than an automatic benign upgrade: attackers and typosquat campaigns both exist in marketplace ecosystems. Grafana Enterprise and Grafana Cloud extend packaging and signing workflows; self-managed fleets must still prove parity across nodes using the same evidence Splunk aggregates here.

RBAC and privacy reviews must settle whether plugin paths, hostnames, and internal Git URLs in error text may land in Splunk. Service accounts that poll /api/plugins per node should use least privilege Grafana tokens, not admin passwords on cron hosts. Break-glass plugin installs performed on a single node during incident response will skew versions until synchronized; annotate those windows with change_ticket_id on forwarded events when your pipeline supports it.

Risk briefing: intentional canary nodes may carry newer plugin builds under approved pilots; lookups should tag canary hosts so severity downshifts without muting production regressions. Rolling upgrades that restart one pod at a time will temporarily show version skew; measure dwell time with streamstats style windows or maintenance metadata before paging application teams. Vendor marketplace mirrors can lag upstream releases; a signature mismatch might reflect mirror delay rather than compromise when checksums still match the vendor object.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.6.4 focuses on per-node plugin inventory parity, loader failures, signature and publisher posture, compatibility and deprecation signals in logs, internal metrics that reflect plugin counts, and allowlisted provenance comparisons. It does not replace sibling Grafana UCs covering dashboard timing, datasource health, alerting evaluation, login audit, or notifier delivery.

Collector posture: stamp stack or cluster identifiers consistently as grafana_stack across logs, metrics, and REST poll results so multisearch fusion does not double-count unrelated environments. Clock skew between poll jobs and Grafana logs widens correlation uncertainty; keep NTP discipline on forwarders and poll workers.

Field normalization expectations: preserve logger, pluginID, version, signature, signatureType, signatureOrg, host, error, path, and level when parsers extract structured JSON from Grafana logfmt or logfmt-like lines. REST inventory events should flatten nested JSON into the same friendly names before indexing as sourcetype=grafana:plugins. When OpenTelemetry Collector maps attributes, align attribute keys to these friendly names with FIELDALIAS.

Hardware and deployment lenses: bare-metal pairs, virtual machines behind keepalived, Kubernetes replicas with PersistentVolumeClaims for plugin caches, and mixed Linux editions all appear in customer estates. Windows Grafana hosts are uncommon but possible; normalize host keys to lowercase FQDNs for join stability.

Compliance framing: evidence that plugin bundles match approved publishers supports vulnerability management narratives aligned with OWASP A06 style outdated and tampered component concerns when paired with your CMDB change discipline. This UC produces operational telemetry; legal teams still decide retention and access class.

Training note for reviewers: teach incident commanders to ask for three artifacts on triage: Splunk row for the skewed pluginID, Grafana /api/plugins JSON from each suspect node, and the package checksum or container image digest recorded in GitOps for that plugin version.

Long-horizon maintenance: quarterly validate that grafana_ha_node_baseline.csv still lists every production member behind the VIP, that grafana_plugin_allowlist.csv includes every marketplace plugin you permit, and that synthetic polls survive credential rotation without silent gaps.


### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes Splunk multisearch can fuse in the saved search, plus two CSV knowledge objects for baselines.

Lane one tails Grafana server logs into index=grafana, index=observability, index=platform, or index=main with sourcetype=grafana:server. Teach inputs to retain logger namespaces plugin.loader, plugins/manager, provisioning, and plugin.signature when present. TRUNCATE must survive long error strings with embedded paths. Example high-value fragments include failed to load plugin, plugin signature: invalid, Plugin is unsigned, deprecated, incompatible API version, and Angular plugin references. Use LINE_BREAKER rules appropriate to your log driver whether it is file tail, systemd journal, or container stdout JSON.

Lane two schedules an authenticated HTTP poll of GET /api/plugins against every Grafana HA member at five- to fifteen-minute intervals consistent with API rate limits. Emit one JSON event per poll with sourcetype=grafana:plugins, fields host, grafana_stack, pluginID, version, signature, signatureType, signatureOrg, and enabled flags when exported. Store service account tokens in the secret store your orchestrator uses; never paste tokens into Splunk lookups. When load balancers hide individual nodes, target the per-pod or per-VM admin URLs from inventory rather than only the VIP so comparisons reflect true member state.

Lane three duplicates lane one filters but emphasizes plugin.loader and plugins/manager error level lines so loader failures remain visible even when generic plugin filters are noisy. Correlate with path fields when Grafana cites pluginPath pointing at read-only mount failures or permission denials.

Lane four scrapes Grafana internal metrics text or Prometheus exposition lines with sourcetype=grafana:metrics into metrics-friendly indexes when your scraper multiplexes logs and metrics on one sourcetype, or land prometheus:metrics lines that include grafana_ and plugin substrings. Preserve labels for instance, host, and job.

Lane five queries Splunk metrics indexes with mstats against metric_name values such as grafana_stat_total_plugins and grafana_build_info when Prometheus remote write lands those series. This arm supports cardinality-safe checks that plugin counts move coherently across replicas after upgrades.

Knowledge object one is lookups/grafana_ha_node_baseline.csv with columns host, grafana_stack, expected_grafana_version, owner_team, rack_site, and optional canary_flag for tuning. Refresh weekly from CMDB or Kubernetes state exports.

Knowledge object two is lookups/grafana_plugin_allowlist.csv with columns pluginID, approved_min_version, signatureOrg_expected, supply_chain_note, and optional deprecation_ack_ticket when you intentionally run legacy plugins under waiver.

props.conf guidance: TIMESTAMP_FIELDS should respect Grafana ISO timestamps when present; KV_MODE or JSON parsing paths should extract nested plugin.id and plugin.info.version from API payloads. For logfmt lines, repeat FIELDALIAS maps until test searches show pluginID populated on ninety-five percent of error rows in steady state.

Pre-save validation: index searches over earliest=-1h must return grafana:server rows after enabling inputs; grafana:plugins must show one row per poll per node; intentional removal of a plugin from one lab node should raise ver_dc within two poll intervals; mstats must move after Grafana restarts in non-production when metrics indexes exist.

Dashboards: host a matrix by grafana_stack and pluginID with color thresholds from severity, drilldowns to raw logs, REST JSON, and metrics lines. Optional Splunk ITSI modules may bind KPIs to counts of high severity skew rows per stack.


### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_6_4_grafana_ha_plugin_skew_provenance with a fifteen minute schedule during steady production, dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h, dispatch.latest_time=now, and alert when severity is high. Throttle duplicate grafana_stack, pluginID, host triples for thirty minutes unless allowlist_status escalates to publisher_mismatch or loader_error_flag remains set. Include recommended_action, ver_dc, and signature_status in pager narratives so incident commanders open plugin runbooks instead of generic Grafana home pages.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans grafana:server signature and lifecycle logs, grafana:plugins REST inventory, plugin.loader error lines, grafana:metrics text lines, and mstats internal Grafana counters so one silent lane cannot hide a genuine skew. After union, coalesce absorbs field naming drift across exporters. stats collapses to latest plugin version per member. streamstats provides short-window ordering context for investigations. eventstats computes distinct plugin versions per stack and pluginID to flag skew_flag. case maps loader failures, skew, unsigned signatures, and publisher mismatches against the allowlist into severity tiers. inputlookup joins add expected Grafana core versions and approved publisher identities. A tstats join on Change.dest enriches stacks with related configuration change counts for correlation in reviews, acknowledging that Change.dest keys must align to your CMDB naming standard.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.4 Grafana HA plugin version skew REST inventory loader errors signature provenance earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where like(lr,"%plugin%") AND (like(lr,"%signature%") OR like(lr,"%unsigned%") OR like(lr,"%invalid%") OR like(lr,"%deprecated%") OR like(lr,"%incompatib%") OR like(lr,"%provision%"))
      | eval pluginID=trim(toString(coalesce(pluginID,pluginId,plugin_id,"unknown_plugin")))
      | eval version=trim(toString(coalesce(version,ver,"")))
      | eval logger=trim(toString(coalesce(logger,"")))
      | eval level=trim(toString(coalesce(level,lvl,"")))
      | eval signature=trim(toString(coalesce(signature,"")))
      | eval signatureType=trim(toString(coalesce(signatureType,signature_type,"")))
      | eval signatureOrg=trim(toString(coalesce(signatureOrg,signature_org,"")))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,instance,instanceId,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval error=trim(toString(coalesce(error,msg,message,"")))
      | eval path=trim(toString(coalesce(path,pluginPath,"")))
      | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(stack,cluster,grafana_cluster,env,"default_stack"))))
      | eval lane="grafana_server_sig"
      | eval loader_error_flag=0
      | eval plugin_ver_norm=if(len(version)>0,version,"unknown")
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:plugins earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval pluginID=trim(toString(coalesce(pluginID,pluginId,id,name,"unknown_plugin")))
      | eval version=trim(toString(coalesce(version,"")))
      | eval signature=trim(toString(coalesce(signature,status,"")))
      | eval signatureType=trim(toString(coalesce(signatureType,type,"")))
      | eval signatureOrg=trim(toString(coalesce(signatureOrg,org,signedBy,"")))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,node,instance,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(stack,cluster,grafana_cluster,env,"default_stack"))))
      | eval lane="rest_plugins_api"
      | eval logger="api_inventory"
      | eval level="info"
      | eval error=""
      | eval path=""
      | eval loader_error_flag=0
      | eval plugin_ver_norm=if(len(version)>0,version,"unknown")
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(coalesce(_raw,""))
      | eval lg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(logger,""))))
      | where (like(lr,"%plugin.loader%") OR like(lg,"%plugin.loader%") OR like(lr,"%plugins/manager%")) AND (like(lr,"%error%") OR like(lr,"%fail%") OR like(lr,"%failed to load%"))
      | eval pluginID=trim(toString(coalesce(pluginID,pluginId,plugin_id,"unknown_plugin")))
      | eval version=trim(toString(coalesce(version,"")))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,instance,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(stack,cluster,grafana_cluster,env,"default_stack"))))
      | eval error=substr(lr,1,480)
      | eval path=trim(toString(coalesce(path,pluginPath,"")))
      | eval logger=trim(toString(coalesce(logger,"plugin.loader")))
      | eval level=trim(toString(coalesce(level,"error")))
      | eval signature=""
      | eval signatureType=""
      | eval signatureOrg=""
      | eval lane="plugin_loader_err"
      | eval loader_error_flag=1
      | eval plugin_ver_norm=if(len(version)>0,version,"unknown")
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics) sourcetype=grafana:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where like(lr,"%plugin%") AND like(lr,"%grafana_%")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,hostname,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(stack,cluster,grafana_cluster,env,"default_stack"))))
      | eval pluginID="_metrics_probe"
      | eval plugin_ver_norm="na"
      | eval signature=""
      | eval signatureType=""
      | eval signatureOrg=""
      | eval logger="internal_metrics"
      | eval level="info"
      | eval error=substr(lr,1,240)
      | eval path=""
      | eval lane="grafana_metrics_line"
      | eval loader_error_flag=0
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=prometheus_metrics metric_name IN ("grafana_stat_total_plugins","grafana_build_info") earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=15m BY host metric_name
      | eval grafana_stack="default_stack"
      | eval pluginID=if(metric_name=="grafana_stat_total_plugins","_internal_plugin_count","_build_info")
      | eval plugin_ver_norm=toString(round(mv,4))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval signature=""
      | eval signatureType=metric_name
      | eval signatureOrg=""
      | eval logger="mstats"
      | eval level="info"
      | eval error=""
      | eval path=""
      | eval lane="mstats_grafana_internal"
      | eval loader_error_flag=0
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
| eval pluginID=coalesce(pluginID,"unknown_plugin")
| eval plugin_ver_norm=coalesce(plugin_ver_norm,"unknown")
| eval signatureOrg=coalesce(signatureOrg,"")
| stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(plugin_ver_norm) AS plugin_ver latest(signatureOrg) AS sig_org latest(signatureType) AS sig_type latest(signature) AS sig_state values(lane) AS lanes max(loader_error_flag) AS loader_error_flag earliest(_time) AS first_seen BY grafana_stack host pluginID
| sort grafana_stack pluginID host - last_seen
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f last(plugin_ver) AS prev_ver BY grafana_stack pluginID host
| eventstats dc(plugin_ver) AS ver_dc values(host) AS member_hosts BY grafana_stack pluginID
| eval skew_flag=if(ver_dc>1,1,0)
| eval lanes_joined=mvjoin(lanes,"|")
| eval signature_status=case(loader_error_flag==1,"loader_fail", match(lower(sig_state),"invalid|unsigned"),"unsigned_or_invalid", like(lanes_joined,"%rest_plugins_api%") AND len(sig_org)==0,"rest_missing_org", true(),"review_sig")
| join type=left max=0 host [| inputlookup grafana_ha_node_baseline.csv | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host))) | fields host expected_grafana_version owner_team rack_site]
| join type=left max=0 pluginID [| inputlookup grafana_plugin_allowlist.csv | eval pluginID=trim(toString(pluginID)) | fields pluginID approved_min_version signatureOrg_expected supply_chain_note]
| eval allowlist_status=case(isnull(signatureOrg_expected),"no_allowlist_row", signatureOrg!=signatureOrg_expected AND len(signatureOrg_expected)>0 AND len(sig_org)>0,"publisher_mismatch", true(),"allowlist_ok_or_unknown")
| eval severity=case(loader_error_flag==1 AND pluginID!="unknown_plugin","high", skew_flag==1,"high", allowlist_status=="publisher_mismatch","high", match(lower(sig_state),"unsigned|invalid"),"medium", ver_dc>2,"high", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="high" AND skew_flag==1,"resync_plugin_bundle_all_ha_members_and_verify_package_checksums", severity=="high" AND loader_error_flag==1,"inspect_plugin_path_permissions_and_grafana_version_matrix", allowlist_status=="publisher_mismatch","verify_plugin_download_source_and_compare_signature_org_to_catalog", true(),"spot_check_rest_inventory_against_filesystem")
| join type=left max=0 grafana_stack [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS related_change_events FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change.All_Changess earliest=-168h@h latest=now BY Change.dest | rename Change.dest AS grafana_stack | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(grafana_stack)))]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval expected_grafana_version=coalesce(expected_grafana_version,"unset_baseline")
| fillnull value=0 related_change_events
| table grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver ver_dc skew_flag loader_error_flag signature_status allowlist_status severity recommended_action last_seen owner_team related_change_events
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_6_4_grafana_ha_plugin_skew_provenance_alert]
cron_schedule = */15 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-observability-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Grafana plugin HA skew $result.severity$ $result.grafana_stack$ $result.pluginID$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_6_4_grafana_ha_plugin_skew_provenance | where severity IN ("high","medium")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal targets, materialize grafana:plugins poll results into a summary index keyed on grafana_stack, host, and pluginID, widen detection windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For very large plugin catalogs, filter allowlist-driven pluginID sets in the REST exporter before indexing.

#### Understanding this SPL: the first arm captures signature policy and deprecation narratives in server logs. The second arm grounds truth for installed versions per node from the API. The third arm isolates loader failures that often precede user-visible panel errors. The fourth arm preserves low-cardinality metrics text for plugin-related internal series. The fifth arm uses mstats when Prometheus stores Grafana self-metrics. Downstream stats and eventstats translate rows into ver_dc skew math that matches operations intuition about phantom panel defects.


### Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on-cluster. For each Splunk row with skew_flag=1, curl -H Authorization Bearer token https://nodeX:3000/api/plugins and compare the version field for pluginID against the Splunk plugin_ver column. Repeat for every member behind the load balancer until versions match or you document an approved canary.

Loader failure validation: grep or journalctl Grafana on the suspect host for plugins/manager logger lines at error level and confirm the path and pluginID cited match Splunk error text. Fix permissions or resync bundles, then confirm loader_error_flag clears within one scheduled search.

Signature validation: open Grafana plugin administration pages or the JSON inventory and confirm signatureType and signatureOrg align with vendor documentation for that release. When Grafana reports unsigned while policy demands signed bundles, treat as governance drift rather than ignoring the warning.

Metrics cross-check: when mstats shows divergent grafana_stat_total_plugins between replicas, confirm Prometheus targets scrape every pod IP rather than only the service VIP, which can hide per-replica truth.

Synthetic positive test: in lab, install mismatched plugin ZIP versions on two Grafana containers behind a test load balancer, confirm high severity within two intervals with non-null recommended_action.

Synthetic negative test: after synchronizing bundles and restarting members, confirm ver_dc returns to one for tested pluginIDs and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to high only.

Runbook linkage: document expected temporary skew during rolling image updates so validators consult change calendars before filing defects.

Negative controls: if REST polls fail with HTTP 401 due to secret rotation, Splunk may falsely show missing inventory; monitor poll health separately.


### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Clock panel version skew across two HA members: compare REST inventory for grafana-clock-panel, redeploy the same ZIP digest to both nodes, restart Grafana in a controlled order, and verify load balancer tests hit both members successfully.

Case 2 — plugin.loader failed to load plugin after NFS glitch: remount shared plugin volume, reconcile fsck or storage events, validate POSIX permissions on pluginPath, and replay bundle copy from artifact registry.

Case 3 — Invalid signature after manual unzip: remove hand-edited trees, reinstall from signed ZIP using Grafana CLI or provisioning job, and update allowlist notes with the correct signatureOrg.

Case 4 — Unsigned plugin warnings in production: either obtain a signed marketplace build, retire the plugin, or document a formal waiver with supply_chain_note in the lookup and governance approval.

Case 5 — Publisher mismatch between 1.9.0 and 1.9.1: pause auto-upgrade jobs, verify download URLs against vendor checksum pages, and open a security review before promoting the new build fleet-wide.

Case 6 — Deprecated Angular app plugin still in dashboards: inventory dashboards referencing the pluginID, plan migration to React replacements, and gate severity until product owners retire panels.

Case 7 — Compatibility error immediately after Grafana core upgrade: read Grafana release notes matrix, upgrade plugin bundles to supported combinations, and roll back core only under explicit change control if required.

Case 8 — Phantom panel errors only on one node: drain that member from the load balancer, compare disk checksums for plugin directories, and resync from the golden image or GitOps artifact.

Case 9 — REST poll blind spot when Kubernetes DNS rotates pod IPs: point polls at stable headless service endpoints or sidecar localhost targets and refresh grafana_ha_node_baseline.csv with current pod names.

Case 10 — mstats arm empty while logs show skew: repair Prometheus scrape or remote-write paths for Grafana self-metrics before muting skew logic that still appears in REST lanes.

Case 11 — False calm when canary host intentionally diverges: mark canary_flag in baseline lookup and tune severity so production members still page while canary experiments continue.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, inputlookup, join, and tstats appear; closing table lists fourteen analyst-visible columns for routing exports.


## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.4 Grafana HA plugin version skew REST inventory loader errors signature provenance earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where like(lr,"%plugin%") AND (like(lr,"%signature%") OR like(lr,"%unsigned%") OR like(lr,"%invalid%") OR like(lr,"%deprecated%") OR like(lr,"%incompatib%") OR like(lr,"%provision%"))
      | eval pluginID=trim(toString(coalesce(pluginID,pluginId,plugin_id,"unknown_plugin")))
      | eval version=trim(toString(coalesce(version,ver,"")))
      | eval logger=trim(toString(coalesce(logger,"")))
      | eval level=trim(toString(coalesce(level,lvl,"")))
      | eval signature=trim(toString(coalesce(signature,"")))
      | eval signatureType=trim(toString(coalesce(signatureType,signature_type,"")))
      | eval signatureOrg=trim(toString(coalesce(signatureOrg,signature_org,"")))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,instance,instanceId,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval error=trim(toString(coalesce(error,msg,message,"")))
      | eval path=trim(toString(coalesce(path,pluginPath,"")))
      | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(stack,cluster,grafana_cluster,env,"default_stack"))))
      | eval lane="grafana_server_sig"
      | eval loader_error_flag=0
      | eval plugin_ver_norm=if(len(version)>0,version,"unknown")
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:plugins earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval pluginID=trim(toString(coalesce(pluginID,pluginId,id,name,"unknown_plugin")))
      | eval version=trim(toString(coalesce(version,"")))
      | eval signature=trim(toString(coalesce(signature,status,"")))
      | eval signatureType=trim(toString(coalesce(signatureType,type,"")))
      | eval signatureOrg=trim(toString(coalesce(signatureOrg,org,signedBy,"")))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,node,instance,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(stack,cluster,grafana_cluster,env,"default_stack"))))
      | eval lane="rest_plugins_api"
      | eval logger="api_inventory"
      | eval level="info"
      | eval error=""
      | eval path=""
      | eval loader_error_flag=0
      | eval plugin_ver_norm=if(len(version)>0,version,"unknown")
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=main) sourcetype=grafana:server earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(coalesce(_raw,""))
      | eval lg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(logger,""))))
      | where (like(lr,"%plugin.loader%") OR like(lg,"%plugin.loader%") OR like(lr,"%plugins/manager%")) AND (like(lr,"%error%") OR like(lr,"%fail%") OR like(lr,"%failed to load%"))
      | eval pluginID=trim(toString(coalesce(pluginID,pluginId,plugin_id,"unknown_plugin")))
      | eval version=trim(toString(coalesce(version,"")))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,instance,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(stack,cluster,grafana_cluster,env,"default_stack"))))
      | eval error=substr(lr,1,480)
      | eval path=trim(toString(coalesce(path,pluginPath,"")))
      | eval logger=trim(toString(coalesce(logger,"plugin.loader")))
      | eval level=trim(toString(coalesce(level,"error")))
      | eval signature=""
      | eval signatureType=""
      | eval signatureOrg=""
      | eval lane="plugin_loader_err"
      | eval loader_error_flag=1
      | eval plugin_ver_norm=if(len(version)>0,version,"unknown")
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform OR index=metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics) sourcetype=grafana:metrics earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(_raw)
      | where like(lr,"%plugin%") AND like(lr,"%grafana_%")
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,hostname,"unknown_host"))))
      | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(stack,cluster,grafana_cluster,env,"default_stack"))))
      | eval pluginID="_metrics_probe"
      | eval plugin_ver_norm="na"
      | eval signature=""
      | eval signatureType=""
      | eval signatureOrg=""
      | eval logger="internal_metrics"
      | eval level="info"
      | eval error=substr(lr,1,240)
      | eval path=""
      | eval lane="grafana_metrics_line"
      | eval loader_error_flag=0
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
    [ | mstats latest(_value) AS mv WHERE index=prometheus_metrics metric_name IN ("grafana_stat_total_plugins","grafana_build_info") earliest=-4h@h latest=now span=15m BY host metric_name
      | eval grafana_stack="default_stack"
      | eval pluginID=if(metric_name=="grafana_stat_total_plugins","_internal_plugin_count","_build_info")
      | eval plugin_ver_norm=toString(round(mv,4))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval signature=""
      | eval signatureType=metric_name
      | eval signatureOrg=""
      | eval logger="mstats"
      | eval level="info"
      | eval error=""
      | eval path=""
      | eval lane="mstats_grafana_internal"
      | eval loader_error_flag=0
      | fields _time grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver_norm signature signatureType signatureOrg logger level error path lane loader_error_flag ]
| eval pluginID=coalesce(pluginID,"unknown_plugin")
| eval plugin_ver_norm=coalesce(plugin_ver_norm,"unknown")
| eval signatureOrg=coalesce(signatureOrg,"")
| stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(plugin_ver_norm) AS plugin_ver latest(signatureOrg) AS sig_org latest(signatureType) AS sig_type latest(signature) AS sig_state values(lane) AS lanes max(loader_error_flag) AS loader_error_flag earliest(_time) AS first_seen BY grafana_stack host pluginID
| sort grafana_stack pluginID host - last_seen
| streamstats window=2 current=t global=f last(plugin_ver) AS prev_ver BY grafana_stack pluginID host
| eventstats dc(plugin_ver) AS ver_dc values(host) AS member_hosts BY grafana_stack pluginID
| eval skew_flag=if(ver_dc>1,1,0)
| eval lanes_joined=mvjoin(lanes,"|")
| eval signature_status=case(loader_error_flag==1,"loader_fail", match(lower(sig_state),"invalid|unsigned"),"unsigned_or_invalid", like(lanes_joined,"%rest_plugins_api%") AND len(sig_org)==0,"rest_missing_org", true(),"review_sig")
| join type=left max=0 host [| inputlookup grafana_ha_node_baseline.csv | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host))) | fields host expected_grafana_version owner_team rack_site]
| join type=left max=0 pluginID [| inputlookup grafana_plugin_allowlist.csv | eval pluginID=trim(toString(pluginID)) | fields pluginID approved_min_version signatureOrg_expected supply_chain_note]
| eval allowlist_status=case(isnull(signatureOrg_expected),"no_allowlist_row", signatureOrg!=signatureOrg_expected AND len(signatureOrg_expected)>0 AND len(sig_org)>0,"publisher_mismatch", true(),"allowlist_ok_or_unknown")
| eval severity=case(loader_error_flag==1 AND pluginID!="unknown_plugin","high", skew_flag==1,"high", allowlist_status=="publisher_mismatch","high", match(lower(sig_state),"unsigned|invalid"),"medium", ver_dc>2,"high", true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="high" AND skew_flag==1,"resync_plugin_bundle_all_ha_members_and_verify_package_checksums", severity=="high" AND loader_error_flag==1,"inspect_plugin_path_permissions_and_grafana_version_matrix", allowlist_status=="publisher_mismatch","verify_plugin_download_source_and_compare_signature_org_to_catalog", true(),"spot_check_rest_inventory_against_filesystem")
| join type=left max=0 grafana_stack [| tstats summariesonly=t count AS related_change_events FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change.All_Changess earliest=-168h@h latest=now BY Change.dest | rename Change.dest AS grafana_stack | eval grafana_stack=lower(trim(toString(grafana_stack)))]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval expected_grafana_version=coalesce(expected_grafana_version,"unset_baseline")
| fillnull value=0 related_change_events
| table grafana_stack host pluginID plugin_ver ver_dc skew_flag loader_error_flag signature_status allowlist_status severity recommended_action last_seen owner_team related_change_events
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count AS change_evt latest(Change.action) AS change_action FROM datamodel=Change WHERE nodename=Change.All_Changess earliest=-168h@h latest=now BY Change.dest Change.object
| rename Change.dest AS change_dest Change.object AS change_object
| table change_dest change_object change_action change_evt
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by grafana_stack, pluginID, and host with drilldowns to grafana:server loader lines, grafana:plugins JSON, grafana:metrics text, mstats plugin count series, and Change enrichment counts; single-value tiles for ver_dc maxima per stack.

## Known False Positives

Rolling Grafana upgrades produce short-lived plugin version skew while StatefulSets or DaemonSets restart members one by one; require dwell thresholds or maintenance tags before paging. Intentional canary nodes and dark-launch hosts may legitimately run newer plugin builds; map them in grafana_ha_node_baseline.csv so severity downshifts without hiding production drift. REST poll failures after token rotation or mutual TLS changes can empty grafana:plugins lanes while logs remain healthy; monitor poll success separately. Shared read-only plugin volumes that lag replication briefly can trigger loader errors during mount storms; correlate with storage events before assuming compromise. Developer laptops forwarding logs into shared indexes can inject unsigned experimental plugins; segregate non-production indexes or filter by grafana_stack. Marketplace mirrors occasionally lag upstream signature metadata even when ZIP checksums match vendor sites; pair Splunk rows with object-store ETag verification. High-cardinality dashboard imports can increase poll payload size until HTTP timeouts look like skew; tune poll intervals and gzip. Duplicate forwarders can double-count REST events until dedupe macros land; compare host cardinality to kube endpoints. Grafana feature toggles after major upgrades change log verbosity for Angular deprecation warnings; revisit extraction rules quarterly. mstats arms go quiet when Prometheus relabel rules drop instance labels; repair scrape configs before muting metrics-based hints.
